name: ob-conversion-rules
description: Conversion strategy principles for Web2App onboarding before paywall. Progress rendering and navigation mechanics are owned by runtime adapters.
mentalModel: Treat the entire OB as a conversion-focused pre-sale experience. The user completes OB, sees the paywall, pays, creates or continues an account, is guided to download the app, and only then experiences the real app.

rules.description: The onboarding flow should be written and structured like a direct-response advertorial for a personalized plan, not a neutral survey. Every page should build belief, trust, plan ownership, or paywall readiness.

Product strategy, page architecture, copy, design, and result pages must ladder toward paywall conversion. The user should believe the paid plan can realistically improve their life before seeing the offer.

Every OB question or input page before result/paywall should belong to a purposeful section. Sections are product-specific and may include Basic Info, Goals, Activity, Diet, Body Metrics, Lifestyle, Barriers, Plan Setup, and Results.
Insert intro_page screens between major sections when they help transition, explain why the next questions matter, reinforce product promise, or build trust. Intro pages do not collect user input.
Intro pages may be pure copy pages, but they are not purposeless. Their purpose is momentum, reassurance, education, objection handling, identity reinforcement, or trust building.

Standard Web2App funnels should use answer-triggered intro_page screens after high-signal answers such as age, primary goal, biggest barrier, and measurements. These pages should turn user answers into trust, social proof, reassurance, or plan ownership.

The onboarding should create a clear current-versus-desired-state gap before sensitive inputs and paywall. Ask where the user is now, where they want to be, and what has blocked them before presenting analysis or the paid plan.
Copy may use direct-response advertising framing and aspirational language, but it must remain plausible, non-medical, non-diagnostic, and not guarantee a specific outcome.
Build trust and personalization value before asking sensitive measurements such as height, current weight, target weight, health limitations, or email.
Summary, analysis, plan generation, and plan ready pages must make users feel the plan was created from their answers before the paywall appears.
After payment, the flow should confirm unlock, create or continue account identity, and guide users toward app download or app continuation.

fitness.purpose: Identify who the user is and make the first interaction low friction.
Collect age, identity, current-state, and desired-state signals while keeping the first questions low friction.
Capture the outcome the user wants and frame the product around it.
Understand current fitness level, routines, preferences, and training constraints.
Collect body context after trust has been established.
Understand schedule, energy, sleep, hydration, diet, and habits when relevant.
Surface objections and adapt the plan to user limitations.
Configure frequency, time, intensity, equipment, and plan format.
Summarize, analyze, generate, and prepare the personalized plan before paywall.

default.purpose: Identify who the user is and reduce first-step friction.
Understand where the user is starting from.
